scope: LayerZero canonical surface. Targets: (1) ZRO OFT token 0x6985884c4392d348587b19cb9eaaf157f13271cd (same addr on chains 1/10/56/137/8453/42161/43114) — ZRO is BOTH an OFT and an ERC20. (2) ClaimContract 0xd6b6a6701303B5Ea36fa0eDf7389b562d8F894DB (42161). Real-tx pulled via Etherscan v2 txlist (offset 80 each, sort=desc; chains 1+42161 deep pages 2-3 added). Probe via target/debug/v3-harness corpus against registryV2/index.
command: set -a; source crates/integration-tests/.env; set +a; for C in 1 42161 8453 10 56; do curl -s "https://api.etherscan.io/v2/api?chainid=$C&module=account&action=txlist&address=<ZRO|CLAIM>&page=1&offset=80&sort=desc&apikey=$ETHERSCAN_API_KEY"; done; import-dune <raw> --chain C --out imp-C.json; (dedup + pin expect from probe); target/debug/v3-harness corpus --root /tmp/v3diag-final
api_limitation: Free Etherscan v2 key supports ONLY chains 1 and 42161 for txlist. Chains 10/56/8453/137/43114 returned status=0 'Free API access is not supported for this chain'. So real traffic was observable on mainnet (ZRO) + Arbitrum (ZRO + ClaimContract) only. Coverage facts for the other 5 chains are from registryV2/index inspection, not real-tx.

totals._note: totals = golden corpus entries (probe verdicts). 'error' = confirmed coverage GAPS (real user tx that produce got=soft no_declarative_v3_mapper). 'hard'=0 means no decoder bug; gaps are missing-mapper (registry) not broken-decoder.

coverage_matrix._note: Per distinct (contract, selector) observed in real traffic. 'covered' = by-callkey index entry exists AND harness probe got != no_declarative_v3_mapper. count = occurrences in sampled window.

ZRO_OFT_token_0x6985884c.name: transfer(to,amount)
ZRO_OFT_token_0x6985884c.gap: no registryV2/tokens/<chain>/zro.json -> ZRO not in erc20 auto-enumerate -> no erc20 callkey for this addr

ZRO_OFT_token_0x6985884c.name: send((dstEid,to,amountLD,minAmountLD,extraOptions,composeMsg,oftCmd),(nativeFee,lzTokenFee),refundAddress)
ZRO_OFT_token_0x6985884c.count: 0 in sample window (chains 1/42161); real send traffic is mostly on other chains the free key can't reach
ZRO_OFT_token_0x6985884c.decode: declarative, layerzero/oft/send manifest (7 chains). Nested sendParam tuple decodes correctly (verified with synthetic calldata). emit domain=unknown (bridge has no ActionBody domain) -> excluded is the correct verdict; full calldata + value preserved for scope analysis.

ClaimContract_0xd6b6a670_chain42161.gap: uncovered donateAndClaim overload — manifest only has 0xac6ae3ee + 0xcd139742 variants

coverage_soft.fix: add registryV2/tokens/<chain>/zro.json kind=erc20 so erc20 auto-enumerate emits the 4 ERC20 callkeys (approve/transfer/transferFrom + setApprovalForAll N/A) for the ZRO address; index rebuild

coverage_soft.name: donateAndClaim (5-arg overload)
coverage_soft.fix: new manifest layerzero/claim-contract/donateAndClaim-v2@1.0.0 (sibling of donateAndClaim@1.0.0); emit domain=airdrop

off_chain_or_unpullable.reason: free Etherscan v2 key rejects these chains ('Free API access is not supported'). OFT send + ERC20 traffic on these chains is the bulk of real LayerZero ZRO bridging but could not be sampled. send manifest covers all 7 chains; ERC20 gap applies to all 7 too.

not present in the chain1/42161 sampled windows (send volume is cross-chain, mostly on L2s the key can't reach). Verified instead with synthetic ABI-encoded send calldata -> got=excluded(domain=unknown), confirming the manifest decodes the nested sendParam tuple.

summary: CORRECTED. LayerZero real-tx surface has TWO confirmed gap clusters. (1) ZRO ERC20 ops (transfer/approve/transferFrom) are the DOMINANT traffic (~500 of ~560 ZRO tx sampled) and are entirely UNCOVERED — there is no registryV2/tokens/<chain>/zro.json, so the erc20 auto-enumerate never emits ERC20 callkeys for the ZRO address; only the OFT send (0xc7c7f5b3) callkey exists. (2) ClaimContract has multiple OVERLOADED claim/donateAndClaim variants; the manifest covers 4 selectors (0xf9429637 claim, 0xac6ae3ee + 0xcd139742 donateAndClaim/donate, 0x46d7ce37 withdrawDonation) but real traffic also hits 3 uncovered overloads (0x8b2eddf9 donateAndClaim-5arg count~8, 0x18d79d13 claim-3arg, 0xcfaf711d donateAndClaim-4arg). Covered selectors decode correctly: claim/donateAndClaim -> domain=airdrop; OFT send -> domain=unknown (excluded, by design, nested tuple verified via synthetic). NO decoder bugs (hard=0) — all gaps are missing-mapper (registry-fixable). Note: free API key blocks chains 10/56/8453/137/43114, so ZRO ERC20+send volume there is unsampled but inherits the same coverage facts.
